title: Total lactate dehydrogenase calorimetric enzyme activity measurement
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8sdk0","text":"Lactate Dehydrogenase is a key cytoplasmic enzyme that confers the observed Warburg effect in tumors. Its a tetrameric protein composed of combinations of one or two isomers. This protein catalyzes the reversible reaction ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"v7pon","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"abj0e","text":"This reaction is monitored spectrophotometrically by observing the reduction in absorbance of NADH (which is maximal at 420nm) as pyruvate is converted to lactate. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cdgdc","text":"This protocol, details the steps involved in such a calorimetric assay of cells and tissues.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b596o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"vf5d","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"620C4364341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":138,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":[{\"key\":\"9u8ef\",\"text\":\"Source: Wikipedia\",\"type\":\"unstyled\",\"depth\":0,\"inlineStyleRanges\":[],\"entityRanges\":[],\"data\":{}}],\"entityMap\":{}}","mime":"image/png","original_name":"image.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/dsirbaas7.jpg","source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/dsipbaas7.jpg","type":1,"webp_placeholder":"https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/dsiqbaas7.webp","webp_source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/dsinbaas7.webp","width":364}}}}

steps: Reaction buffer – Tris buffer @ pH 7.2 , 200 millimolar (mM) Sodium chloride P212121 , 200 micromolar (µM) β-Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide reduced disodium salt hydrate (NADH) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #N8129 (CAS: 606-68-8)
Measure and add the following: i. 1.57 g HCl Tris ii. 0.15 g Tris Base iii. 1.64 g of NaCl iv. Use solution to dissolve NADH and add 20 mg (10 vials of 2 mg each)
Turn off stir plate and remove stir bar
Store in -20 °C freezer as 12 mL aliquots (for 12 dilutions of pyruvate). This solution will be stable for upto 6 months.
Lysis buffer – 50 Molarity (M) Tris buffer pH 8.2 , 2 millimolar (mM) DTT, 2 millimolar (mM) EDTA, 1% Triton x-100.
Measure and add the following: i. 0.71 g HCl Tris ii. 0.67 g Tris Base iii. 0.15 g EDTA iv. 0.06 g (or 59.326 uL) of DTT v. 2 mL Triton
Store as 200 µL l aliquots in -20 °C C freezer (is stable for up to a year). This is 10 times concentrated, so for the assay add 1800 µL dH 2 O.
Store 500 µL aliquots of sodium pyruvate ( 11 mg/mL ) in the - 20 °C freezer. This solution is stable for up to a year when frozen.
Take from the freezer, one tube each of the pyruvate and reaction buffer and place in 30 °C water bath.Thaw the lysis buffer and add 1800 µL of dH 2 O and place on ice
Trypsinize and collect cells:
Cell Counting
If using a cell counter, use the average of three measurements to determine the number of cells.
If using a hemocytometer (count the cells in 1,2,3 and 4 squares in both chambers and take the average). cells/ml = average count per square × 10 4 Hemocytometer
Total cells = cells/ml × dilution (if used) x total original volume of cell suspension from which sample was taken)
Re-suspend the cells in lysis buffer to obtain a concentration of 10 7 cells/ml

Determine appropriate tissue/cell dilution to be used for the assay. a. Use 0.6 millimolar (mM) pyruvate concentration and plate triplicates of different sample dilutions. b. In a 96-well plate, add 3 µL of each TL:LB dilution in triplicate. c. Add 147 µL of the 0.6 millimolar (mM) pyruvate concentration to each well.
Aspirate the medium from cell culture vessel, and rinse with PBS. Aspirate the PBS, and add 0.25% trypsin in PBS.
Place the culture vessel in the incubator (4-10 minutes based on cell type), and after the cells detach, add media (containing serum to inactivate the trypsin).
Transfer the cells to a 15 mL tube and keep some in an eppendorf tube for cell counting.
Centrifuge 15 mL tubes at 300 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 at 4 °C .
In a 200 mL beaker, measure and add 140 mL dH2O Contributed by users
Place magnetic stir bar in beaker, place onto stir plate and set to 200 rpm, °C,
Measure and record buffer pH (should be pH 7.2 , adjust with appropriate base/acid)
In a 50 mL conical tube, measure and add 20 mL of dH2O

Weigh and record tissue sample ( 6-10 mg is sufficient) carefully using sterile forceps (keep frozen). a. Record any additional information about the sample (sample name/description, locus of tissue taken, date, etc.). b. Place tissue into a screw cap vial (compaitble with the bead homogenizer) and place on ice.

Prepare varying Tissue/Cell lysate : Lysis Buffer (TL:LB) dilutions (subject to change with varying sample type): a.2X ( 100 µL TL : 100 µL LB) b.5X ( 40 µL TL : 160 µL LB) c.10X ( 10 µL TL : 190 µL LB) Keep tissue lysate on ice until ready to pipette into 96 well plate.
Add 500 µL of lysis buffer into the vial with tissue and place on ice

Two options for homogenizing tissue Option 1 - Using bead homogenizer - a. Place a bead (3-5 mm stainless steel) into the bead homogenizer compatible vial (from step 11b) b. Operate the machine for 00:02:00 at 20–30 Hz Option 2 - Using stator-rotor - a. Label plastic tubes EtOH and 0.1% DMPC (dimethyl pyrocarbonate) , one set for each sample) for cleaning. b. Place homogenizer into EtOH for 00:00:10 ; wipe excess with Kimwipe. c. Place homogenizer into DMPC H2O for 00:00:10 and wipe excess with Kimwipe. d. Use homogenizer to grind tissue at medium to high settings for approximately 00:00:30 or until tissue is fully homogenized. e. repeat steps b and c before moving to the next sample to clean the homogenizer
Centrifuge homogenate at 4 °C for 00:05:00 at 7000 rpm, °C,
Transfer supernatant and place into a separate eppendorf tube labeled with sample name.
Set-up the bead homogenizer in the cold room
Set -up buckets of ice and dry-ice as needed

TECAN spectrophotometer on and set temperature at 30 °C . a. Ensure the sample plate map of the appropriate method is set for the assay. b. Set-up the protocol for a dynamic 00:10:00 readout at 420 nm and for the appropriate 96 well plate
Prepare 12 pyruvate concentrations using pyruvate and reaction buffer by carefully pipeting into pre-labeled conical tubes
Example of dilutions (can be subject to change based on sample type and amount of buffer needed): Pyruvate Dilution table
Calculate the amount of pyruvate + reaction buffer dilution needed for the entire assay - 147 µL x 3 (triplicate) x (# samples)
Pipette calculated amount + 150 µL (to ensure enough is available) into each corresponding 12 well multichannel reservoir- from lowest to highest pyruvate concentration.

Do linear regression for each well (change in absorbance over the 10 minutes) to obtain the slope OD/min. (Note: the TECAN Infinity200M accounts automatically for the 1 cm path length. So the measurement is actually OD/min/cm)
Place the 96-well plate in the spectrophotometer and measure immediately.

147 µL Once the measurement has completed in 00:10:00 , Calculate a linear regression for each well to obtain the kinetic output slope (OD/min) and determine the correct dilution by identifying the dilution with the most linear slope.

Pipette 3 µL of appropriate TL:LB dilution into three rows (technical replicates) and 12 columns (corresponding to the 12 pyruvate dilutions) on the 96 micro well plate. Note: Ensure that there are no bubbles
Using a 200 µL multichannel pipettor, carefully pipette 147 µL of each 12 pyruvate dilutions in triplicate; assay immediately. Note: Ensure that there are no bubbles
Place the 96-well plate in the spectrophotometer and measure immediately.
Once the read has completed in 00:10:00 , click Export to excel and save the file. Eject the plate and clean up
Keep the multi-well reservoir in the 30 °C water bath

LDH enzyme ctivity is calculated as uM NADH/min = OD/min (slope) * Solution Volume in each well ( 150 µL ) / ( volume of sample lysate ( 3 µL ) * e ) where e=extinction coeff=0.00622 uM -1 cm -1 Note: take the absolute value of OD/min (omit the negative sign)
Take the average (of the three technical replicates) activity per pyruvate concentration subject to a threshold of Rsq > 0.9
Plot Tissue Activity Michaelis - Menten curve a.X = Pyruvate Concentration (mM) b.Y = Average Activity
For simpler linear curve
plot Lineweaver-Burke: a.X = 1/Pyruvate Concentration b.Y = 1/Average Activity

Obtain trend-line linear fit in the Excel program and calculate a.KM = slope / intercept ; [mM] b.Vmax = 1/ intercept ; uM NADH/min Use Bradford assay kit ( https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/23200#/23200 ) to determine protein concentration to express Vmax (LDH activity) in umols NADH/min/mg of protein
creator: Renuka Sriram
